summary: Loose ’69 set with smoking Lovelight and monster China>Rider jam
blurb: The show delivers a tight eleven‑song set highlighted by Black Peter, a fiery Turn On Your Lovelight, and an extended China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider jam. Jerry’s guitar smokes on the Lovelight while Phil’s bass drives the high‑energy moments. The soundboard capture is clear enough to enjoy despite occasional static and a few cuts.
review:
On December 13, 1969 at Swing Auditorium the Dead rolled out an eleven‑song set that feels both loose and purposeful. Pigpen’s presence looms large, especially on Black Peter and Turn On Your Lovelight, where Tom Constanten adds a crisp organ lick that makes Jerry’s guitar sound “smoking” as noted in the recording notes. The centerpiece is the monster China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider jam, with Phil’s bass lines locking in tight and driving the improvisation forward. Good Lovin’ and Cumberland Blues keep the momentum high, while Saint Stephen slides seamlessly into a rousing Turn On Your Lovelight finale. Although the source misses the encore and has occasional static, the soundboard is generally clear and well‑balanced, making this capture the definitive way to experience the night.

For anyone looking to hear a classic late‑’60s Dead performance—complete with Pigpen’s bluesy grit, Jerry’s soaring leads, and a tight rhythm section—the recommended recording (gd69-12-13.sbd.clugston.5583.sbeok.shnf) offers the best listening experience despite its minor imperfections.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All available analysis is positive, praising standout songs (Black Peter, Lovelight, China Cat > Rider) and band members' performances. The language includes 'smoking', 'monster jam', and notes the set feels loose yet engaging, indicating strong positive sentiment across the recording.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard capture with good balance, showcasing the standout songs and performances despite occasional static.

key_highlights: Black Peter shines with Pigpen’s gritty vocals.
Turn On Your Lovelight features a smoking guitar solo and crisp organ lick.
Monster China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider jam highlights Jerry and Phil's tight interplay.
Overall soundboard quality is clear enough for an enjoyable listen, with only minor static.

band_performance.Jerry: His guitar is described as ‘smoking’ on Lovelight and his vocals are heartfelt and unique.
band_performance.Phil: Bass lines receive praise, especially during the China Cat jam and other high‑energy sections.
band_performance.Brent: Tom Constanten’s organ work stands out, especially the crisp lick during the Lovelight transition.
